smartlead_campaign: OR #6 | Personalized Line | Forecast Lead Magnet | Without Other Contact Name

thread.subject: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria, saw braids and wigs leans on same day shipping plus local delivery in about an hour, curious how that's been landing.

 separate note: I made Braids and Wigs a 4-min ad fatigue report from your live Meta ads. the one about to die, the duplicate eating budget, which to scale, and a fresh angle to test.

 most ecom brands in your niche usually run the same handful of ads too long. this spots it before ROAS drops.

 worth a look?

 Kyle H.
 Co-founder | OmniRocket

 P.S. if there's a better person for this, point me their way :)

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - forgot to mention: the report also catches the ad Meta is quietly throttling. it's usually a near-copy of another ad that's eating budget without you noticing.

 Braids and Wigs's is still sitting ready on my end - want me to send it?

 - Kyle

 P.S. if it's not you, who should I reach out to?

thread.subject: RE: Braids and Wigs ad report
thread.body_text:
Maria - last check on this.

 should I shelve Braids and Wigs's fatigue report, or is there a better week to send it? no worries either way - just don't want to keep landing in your inbox.

 - Kyle

 P.S. if timing's just off, ping me in a few weeks - still happy to send.

Hi Kyle,
 

 Thank you for following up. Before we move forward, could you clarify what you'd need from us, specifically, did you need access to our Meta ad account to put this report together and if so, what level?
 

 Best regards,
 

 
 Maria Mushi 
 Manager – Braids and Wigs 
 London, Ontario 
 226-504-1321 
 www.braidsandwigs.ca

Hi Maria,

 great question, and the short answer is nothing needed on your end for what i already built.

here's the report btw: Braids and Wigs Ad Fatigue Report 

 this report was built entirely from public data. i pulled your live ad from meta's ad library and analyzed them from the outside, so no account access was involved at all.

 access only comes into play for the deeper work: a full audit plus a 30 and 90 day plan mapped to your actual numbers.

 the way we work is we hop on a quick call first, i get to understand your brand and where you're trying to go, and if we're a good fit we move forward. that's the point where i'd need access, and we'd start with read-only.

 worth a 30 min chat to see if it's a fit?

 Book a time here → Calendar 

 - Kyle

copy.tldr: Braids and Wigs is a value-priced Canadian hair retailer running just one weak 'Send message' ad, so the play is to build real avatar-based creative around their headband wigs and hit the free shipping threshold, but their low median price is the thing you must probe before promising anything.

deep_dive.what_they_are: A Canadian online beauty store based in London, Ontario. They sell wigs, braiding hair, extensions, and hair and skin care across many known brands like Sensationnel, Mane Concept, Outre, and Freetress. They also do same day shipping and local delivery within an hour in London.
deep_dive.what_they_sell: 500 products. Prices run from under a dollar up to about $508. A lot of the catalog is small stuff like gels, beads, and wig caps. The bigger ticket items are human hair wigs and bundles, roughly $58 to $145.
deep_dive.positioning: High-variety value play. Name-brand looks at low prices, with buy now pay later and free shipping over $69.99. Not a boutique or premium house.
deep_dive.who_buys: Value-conscious Canadian women who wear protective styles - wigs, braids, weaves, crochet - and want name-brand looks fast, affordably, and with easy payment options.
strengths: Strong offers already in place: free shipping over $69.99, a free gift at $99.99, buy now pay later, and same day or one hour local delivery. These are real reasons to buy that ads can lead with.
Deep catalog of recognized brands, which gives lots of angles and products to test without needing new inventory.
gaps: Only one ad running, and it is a generic store post with a 'Send message' button. That does not sell a product or drive checkout. There is no clear offer, no product, no reason to click.
No reviews anywhere on the site. There is nothing to show a first-time buyer that other people trust this store, which makes cold traffic harder to convert.

game_plan.headline: Turn one vague 'message us' ad into a set of product-led ads built around three real buyer types, aimed at the free shipping cart size.

phases.goal: Find out which buyer type and which angle actually gets sales, not to scale. We want to learn what works before spending more.
campaigns: One sales campaign with three ad sets, one per avatar, all pointing to product or collection pages, not the inbox.
One simple retargeting ad set for people who viewed a product or added to cart but did not buy, leaning on the free shipping and pay later offers.
lead_products: Human Hair Water Wave Headband Wig ($145.10) and Human Hair Kinky Headband Wig ($123.34) - lead with these. They are glueless and 'easy install', which is the strongest single message for a busy first-time buyer, and headband wigs are one of the easiest wig types to sell cold. These are our best guess at hero products because they are the ones the brand chose to feature and describe most fully.

Sensationnel Empire Human Hair Feather Crochet Braids ($58.04) and French Curl Braiding Hair 28in ($5.80) - use these for the braids buyer, and pair the cheap braiding hair with other items to reach the $69.99 free shipping line.
Mane Concept Brown Sugar Wig (on sale $65.29, was $101.57) - use the sale price as the deal angle for the bargain hunter.
phases.creative: Short video or simple before-and-after style images showing the wig on and how fast it goes on. Lead each ad with one clear message per avatar. Show the price and the free shipping and pay later offers on screen. Kill the 'Send message' button and send people to the product page to buy. Test 2 to 3 creatives per avatar.
phases.budget: Start around $50 to $70 CAD a day across the three avatars, then shift budget to whichever one is getting the cheapest sales after about two weeks.
phases.what_success_looks_like: By day 30 we know which avatar and which angle brings in the cheapest sales, we have at least one ad clearly beating the others, and the retargeting ad is bringing back cart abandoners. That is the base to scale from in month two.

estimates.label: Starting budget
estimates.basis: Enough to get roughly 30 to 40 clicks a day at typical costs in this category, spread across three test audiences, which is the floor for learning which one works in 30 days.

estimates.label: Revenue we would aim to add
estimates.value: We would set this after the call once we know the real average order and margin
estimates.basis: The public median product price is about $10.52, which we are using as a stand-in for average order value. If that is the true order size, paid ads will struggle to pay for themselves. Their free shipping line at $69.99 and the $99.99 gift suggest they want bigger baskets, so the real average order is the single number we must confirm before promising any revenue figure.

assumptions: The real average order value is well above the $10.52 median, closer to the $69.99 free shipping line. If most orders are small single items, the whole paid plan changes.
The headband wigs are among their better sellers. If Maria says something else sells more, we lead with that instead.
They can ship reliably and handle more orders. Their same day shipping claim suggests yes.
They are willing to run ads that send people to product pages to buy, not just to message the store.

questions.question: What are you spending on Meta right now, and who is running it?
questions.listening_for: Whether there is any real budget and whether they do it themselves or have an agency. One live 'message us' ad suggests very little is being spent. If budget is tiny, set expectations early.

questions.question: When someone buys from you, what is a normal order size in dollars?
questions.listening_for: This is the key number. If it is near $10 to $20, paid ads are very hard. If it is $70 plus because of the free shipping line, the plan works. Do not guess this for them, get the real answer.

questions.question: On a wig that sells for around $120 to $145, roughly how much is left after you pay for it?
questions.listening_for: Their margin. Value stores selling other brands often run thin. If margin is small, we need higher order values or repeat buyers to make ads pay.

questions.question: Have you run ads that sent people to buy on the site before, and what happened?
questions.listening_for: Past results and past pain. If they tried and it flopped, find out if it was the ad, the offer, or the site. Their only current ad drives messages, not sales, so they may never have tested real buying traffic.

questions.question: When people message that current ad, do those chats turn into sales?
questions.listening_for: Whether the 'Send message' approach works for them or just creates busywork. If chats rarely convert, that supports switching to product-page ads.

questions.question: How much of your business comes from local London delivery versus shipping across Canada?
questions.listening_for: Whether to run a local-only fast delivery angle or a Canada-wide angle. The one hour delivery is a strong local hook but useless for a national audience.

questions.question: Do you have any reviews, photos of real customers, or messages from happy buyers we could use?
questions.listening_for: There are no reviews on the site. If she has any proof sitting in DMs or texts, we can put it in ads. If there is truly none, we build trust another way and flag that ads will convert slower.

questions.question: Which products actually sell the most for you right now?
questions.listening_for: Confirms or corrects our guess that headband wigs are the hero. Their real bestsellers should lead the ads. Do not assume, let her tell you.

questions.question: Do you get many people finding you on Google searching for things like 'headband wig Canada' or 'braiding hair London Ontario'?
questions.listening_for: People clearly search for wigs and braiding hair by name and by city, so this is a real Google and local search opening. Listen for whether they show up now or are invisible. If they are invisible, that is a second project worth mentioning.

questions.question: Is most of your business online across Canada, or is the London store the core?
questions.listening_for: If local matters, local search and Google Maps are strong low-cost adds. If national, search ads on branded product terms fit better. Only push this if the answer gives a real reason.

objections.objection: We already get sales from people messaging us, why change the ad?
objections.answer: That is good to hear, and we would not touch what works until the new ads prove out. But messages take your time and only reach people willing to chat. Ads that send people straight to buy can run while you sleep and reach the ones who just want to check out fast. We test it alongside what you have, we do not rip it out.

You may not need much. Your offers are already strong, free shipping, a free gift, pay later. What is missing is showing a real product with a real reason to buy. We can start around $50 to $70 a day to learn what works, and only spend more once something is clearly paying off.
objections.objection: We do not have a big ad budget.
How do I know ads will make money when I sell at low prices?
objections.answer: That is the exact thing I want to nail down with you. If most orders are small, ads are hard and I will tell you that straight. But your free shipping line at $69.99 and your bigger wigs at $120 plus suggest bigger baskets are normal. Tell me your real average order and I will tell you honestly if the numbers work.

objections.objection: You do not have access to my account, so how can you really help?
objections.answer: For the plan we build first, I do not need it. Everything so far came from your public ad and site. Once we start, I would ask for read-only access so I can see your real numbers and stop guessing. You stay in control the whole time.

red_flags.flag: The public median price is only about $10.52, and much of the catalog is cheap add-ons like gels, beads, and caps.
red_flags.why_it_matters: If real orders are that small, paid ads cannot pay for themselves. We must confirm the true average order value on the call before promising any results. Do not commit to revenue numbers until we know this.

red_flags.flag: No reviews anywhere on the site.
red_flags.why_it_matters: First-time buyers have nothing showing other people trust this store. Cold ad traffic converts slower without social proof. We need to gather reviews or customer photos early, or ads will underperform.

red_flags.flag: Only one live ad and it uses a 'Send message' button, not a buy action.
red_flags.why_it_matters: It tells us they have little paid experience and no proof that buying traffic converts on their site. We are starting close to zero, so set expectations that month one is about learning, not scaling.

red_flags.flag: The reply came from a braidswigs@gmail.com and a personal manager, while the lead email is maria@braidsandwigs.ca.
red_flags.why_it_matters: Small operation with one person wearing many hats. Decisions may be slower and budget tighter. Keep the ask simple and the starting spend low.

other_channel_openings: Google Search: people actively search for wigs, headband wigs, and braiding hair by name, so branded product search ads and shopping could catch buyers already looking. Worth raising only after confirming order value works.

Local search and Google Maps for London, Ontario: their one hour local delivery is a strong hook, and 'braiding hair London Ontario' type searches are real. Low cost way to win nearby buyers if the store is a core part of the business.
